---
title: "Study Families"
subtitle: "Cohort-level view of accessions, modality siblings, and counting status"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    toc: true
---

<div class="page-intro">
This view treats the study family as the primary unit. A family may contain one accession, multiple modality-specific accessions, a public archive row, a portal mirror, or an integrated-resource component. Use this page before counting cohorts, comparing modalities, or deciding whether two rows are independent.
</div>

## Study-Family Explorer

<div id="study-family-explorer"></div>

---

## Modality Matrix

<div class="page-intro">
The matrix marks whether a study family currently has evidence for each omics layer in the registry. A checked modality means the family has at least one row or row annotation for that layer; it does not mean every modality is analysis-ready or sample-matched.
</div>

<div id="study-modality-matrix"></div>

---

<div class="disclaimer">
<strong>Counting rule.</strong> Families with sibling accessions should be counted once at the cohort level unless row-level evidence proves that the accessions represent independent participant sets. Rows marked <em>Unclear</em> remain visible for manual review.
</div>
